channels.title: Does the kernel need its own chat surface, or do MCP + CLI + the terminal cover it?
channels.summary: Settled twice in one week: the chat pane, then the embedded terminal, both failed the deletion test against MCP + CLI coverage. Surfaces settle at MCP + CLI.

channels.title: What stands between this repo and being a credible market entrant?
channels.summary: Six repo-verified gaps between the kernel and a credible market entry. License (MIT) and packaging closed; the product name and repo visibility remain operator calls.

channels.title: What must be true before 1.0 — when is the tool ready to instance elsewhere?
channels.summary: The five 1.0 criteria: instance birth in the test suite (done), seven unattended days, a real-data loop in a born instance, a cold-start human test, all gates green at tag.

channels.title: How far should the brain adopt Phoenix-Architecture concepts into its schema?
channels.summary: Which further Phoenix-Architecture concepts (deletion test, provenance-over-diffs, regeneration drills) earn schema weight beyond constraints.md and implementation-memory.md. Open.

channels.title: How the three ideas compose — a conversational, provenance-aware knowledge graph grounded in deterministic facts
channels.summary: Original synthesis: the three ideas are "one loop bound by provenance." A five-persona RFC (2026-07-14) rejected that framing as an overfit narrative — the pieces touch disjoint graphs and the real bus is the inbox, which already exists. Revised verdict: three independent bets, not an epic. The connector (drift) is the only latent-valuable one and is multiply gated; the conversation surface is a no-go (re-litigates a settled decision + completes a prompt-injection trifecta); provenance tags proceed standalone with a corrected design.
